source_jobs.title: Senior AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLANK METAL

Blank Metal is an AI-native engineering company that partners with enterprises to deliver production AI fast. We blend strategic insight with hands-on execution, shipping high-impact systems that turn AI initiatives into measurable business results.

We're builders, not theorists. Our team of seasoned engineers, designers, and strategists work across industries to create AI applications that actually get used and make a difference.




THE ROLE

You're a technical leader who shapes how Blank Metal approaches AI engineering. With experience designing, building, and shipping AI features that hold up under real‑world use, you combine deep technical expertise with mentorship and vision. You architect complex AI systems, make critical technical decisions, and elevate the entire team's capabilities.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead the technical architecture and strategy for AI‑powered projects

 - Design scalable, production‑grade systems that handle real‑world complexity

 - Make critical decisions about tooling, frameworks, and architectural patterns

 - Own the quality and reliability of systems in production

 - Mentor and develop other engineers

 - Drive technical discussions and shape team standards

 - Keep up with emerging tools and techniques

 - Build observability and evaluation frameworks

 - Partner with product and client teams to translate business needs into technical solutions

 - Contribute to longer‑term technical vision and innovation


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - AI Architecture Expertise: Deep understanding of production AI systems design, scalability, and reliability

 - Production AI Leadership: Extensive hands-on experience building, deploying, and operating AI systems

 - System Design Mastery: Can architect complex AI integrations that scale, handle edge cases, and provide excellent observability

 - AI Tooling Depth: Expert knowledge of LLM APIs, vector databases, prompt engineering, agents, and emerging AI tools

 - Full-Stack Fluency: Advanced development skills across frontend, backend, and infrastructure

 - AI Observability & Evals: Experience building evaluation frameworks and monitoring for AI systems

 - Technical Leadership: You mentor others, set technical standards, and drive architectural decisions

 - Strategic Thinking: You connect technical decisions to business outcomes

 - Learning Mindset: You stay current with the rapidly evolving AI landscape


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - At least 5 years of professional software engineering experience, with a demonstrated ability to deliver production‑ready AI features that real users depend on

 - Proven record of shipping complex, mission‑critical software

 - Strong full‑stack and/or backend development skills applied to real projects

 - Experience designing and leading technically challenging work from concept through delivery

 - Shown ability to guide others, share knowledge, and influence technical direction


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience building RAG systems at scale

 - Deep experience with vector databases and semantic search

 - Background in AI observability, evaluation frameworks, or monitoring

 - Experience with agent-based architectures and agentic AI systems

 - Understanding of inference optimization and model serving

 - Previous mentorship or technical leadership roles

 - Contribution to AI-focused open source projects


WHY YOU'LL LOVE IT HERE

 - Build AI that ships: every project ends in a live deployment, not a slide deck

 - Craft meets velocity: we obsess over quality while delivering at startup speed

 - Outcome-driven model: our projects align with client success, not billable hours

 - AI-native culture: you'll work with peers who deeply understand modern AI's potential and limits

 - Real collaboration: open conversations, shared ownership, and space to bring your voice and creativity


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In your role, you'll:

 - Establish technical standards and best practices for AI engineering across

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Blank Metal
Blank Metal is an AI-native engineering company that partners with enterprises to deliver production AI fast. We blend strategic insight with hands-on execution — shipping high-impact systems that turn AI initiatives into measurable business results.


We’re builders, not theorists. Our team of seasoned engineers, designers, and strategists work across industries to create AI applications that actually get used — and make a difference.

You’ll join a seasoned professional services team known for high-touch, high-quality work that drives real impact. Our culture thrives on open dialogue, collaborative problem-solving, and a healthy dose of laughter. We value your voice, your ideas, and your entrepreneurial spirit—come help us evolve our services and shape our journey.

Key Responsibilities

 - Design scalable service/micro-service patterns, database schemas, caching layers, and authentication flows

 - Ship production-grade code across front-end (React/TypeScript) and back-end (Node, Go, or JVM) with strong test coverage

 - Integrate model endpoints, manage real-time inference, and implement robust error-handling and fallback mechanisms

 - Drive code-review rigor, CI/CD pipeline health, and observability instrumentation

 - Pair-program, lead brown-bag sessions, and advise junior engineers on technical decisions

 - Collaborate with UX/Product, AI/ML Practice, and ML Ops teams to deliver client solutions

 - Contribute to engineering best practices and help raise the team’s technical bar

About you

 - 5–8 years of full-stack development experience, with strength in both modern front-end and cloud-native back-end stacks

 - Experience designing and operating systems in AWS, GCP, or Azure using Infrastructure as Code (Terraform/CDK)

 - Solid understanding of security, performance tuning, and API versioning best practices

 - Comfortable in dynamic, client-facing environments—startup or consulting experience preferred

 - Strong communicator and collaborator with a passion for building high-impact solutions

 - Curious, adaptable, and energized by solving complex problems across industries

Why You’ll Love It Here

 - Build AI that ships — every project ends in a live deployment, not a slide deck

 - Craft meets velocity — we obsess over quality while delivering at startup speed

 - Outcome-driven model — our projects align with client success, not billable hours

 - AI-native culture — you’ll work with peers who deeply understand modern AI’s potential and limits

 - Backed to build — supported by $3M in seed funding from Rally Ventures, Traction
   Capital, and PurePlay Partners

 - Real collaboration — open conversations, shared ownership, and space to bring your voice and creativity
   

Equal Opportunity

Blank Metal is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We welcome all backgrounds and perspectives. Diversity makes our work better and our team stronger.



Blank Metal is an equal opportunity employer.

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLANK METAL

Blank Metal is an AI-native engineering company that partners with enterprises to deliver production AI fast. We blend strategic insight with hands-on execution, shipping high-impact systems that turn AI initiatives into measurable business results.

We're builders, not theorists. Our team of seasoned engineers, designers, and strategists work across industries to create AI applications that actually get used and make a difference.




THE ROLE

You're an experienced engineer who ships production AI features with confidence. You understand how to integrate LLM APIs and AI services into robust applications, architect scalable AI-powered systems, and deliver high-impact features that clients depend on.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and implement end-to-end AI integrations: from LLM API selection to production deployment

 - Architect AI-powered features that work reliably at scale

 - Work independently on complex projects with minimal oversight

 - Leverage LLM frameworks, vector databases, and prompt engineering to solve real problems

 - Integrate cloud AI services and build custom AI tooling when needed

 - Ship production-grade code that balances quality and velocity

 - Collaborate with designers, product managers, and other engineers

 - Contribute to technical decisions and architectural discussions

 - Mentor junior engineers when needed


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Production Expertise: Proven ability to ship production AI features end-to-end

 - LLM Mastery: Deep hands-on experience with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and frameworks like LangChain

 - Full-Stack Skills: Strong development across frontend (React/modern frameworks), backend (Python, Node.js, .NET), and infrastructure

 - System Design: Can architect AI integrations that scale, handle errors gracefully, and provide good user experiences

 - AI Tooling: Comfortable with vector databases, prompt engineering, embedding models, and emerging AI tools

 - Quality Focus: You care about code quality, testing, and building systems that users can rely on

 - Initiative: You identify problems, propose solutions, and drive projects to completion


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - 2-5 years of professional software engineering experience

 - Demonstrated production AI experience (LLM integrations, AI-powered features)

 - Advanced full-stack development skills

 - Experience with modern cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)

 - Strong backend and infrastructure development skills applied to real production systems

 - Proven ability to ship complex features independently




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems

 - Familiarity with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, etc.)

 - Experience contributing to frontend features using modern frameworks when needed

 - Background with API design and system integration

 - Understanding of prompt engineering best practices

 - Experience with deployment and DevOps practices

 - Exposure to agentic AI systems


WHY YOU'LL LOVE IT HERE

 - Build AI that ships: every project ends in a live deployment, not a slide deck

 - Craft meets velocity: we obsess over quality while delivering at startup speed

 - Outcome-driven model: our projects align with client success, not billable hours

 - AI-native culture: you'll work with peers who deeply understand modern AI's potential and limits

 - Real collaboration: open conversations, shared ownership, and space to bring your voice and creativity


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In your role, you'll:

 - Ship production AI features with minimal guidance

 - Own the technical direction of AI integrations on your projects

 - Become the go-to expert on AI architecture and tooling

 - Help raise the bar for how the team approaches AI engineering

 - Build a track record of high-impact, reliable AI systems

 - Grow as a leader by mentoring other engineers




EQUAL OPPORTUNITY

Blank Metal is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. W

source_jobs.title: AI Workflow Engineer
source_jobs.description:
DEPARTMENT Enablement

LOCATION Minneapolis, MN (Hybrid)

TYPE Full time

REPORTS TO Head of Enablement



THE ROLE

This is a new role in a new practice. You’ll help enterprise clients take their work apart and put it back together using tools from our partners, Anthropic and OpenAI.

Workflow Engineers discover, design, build, and teach AI-enabled workflows. You walk into an organization, run a training session that changes how they work, help them redesign the workflows that matter most, and build the skills, plugins, and playbooks that let their teams keep going after you’re gone.

We expect you to own things: curriculum, methodology, delivery approach. Proactively problem-solve when the approach isn’t working.



WHAT YOU GET TO DO

Four areas. The mix flexes by engagement and by quarter as the practice scales.

Enablement Delivery

 - Design and deliver training — from half-day leadership sessions to multi-week engagements.

 - Facilitate workshops that help technical and non-technical teams build real confidence with the tools, then measure whether it stuck.

 - Coach client champions through adoption, not just tool proficiency.

 - Spot follow-on opportunities during and after training. Surface them to Account Leads with enough context to scope and close.

Workflow Design & Build with Champions

 - Take work apart. Understand what's actually happening before you redesign anything.

 - Redesign workflows around partner tools, with the human in the loop.

 - Build skills, plugins, playbooks, and connector patterns alongside clients — not for them.

 - Document the redesign so it can be replicated across teams and departments.

Discovery & Use Case Development

 - Partner with clients to identify high-value AI use cases and build the business case.

 - Lead discovery workshops and use case prioritization sessions using our Blueprint scoring framework.

 - Collaborate with Account Leads to scope engagements and define success metrics.

Practice Development

 - Own our partner enablement methodology: curriculum, session playbooks, assessment tools, certification pathways.

 - Create reusable workflow patterns and training assets so we don't rebuild every engagement from scratch.

 - Define what good looks like: quality standards, client outcomes, adoption signals we can track and feed back into the curriculum.

 - Support pre-sales: scoping, proposal development, helping Account Leads explain what an engagement actually delivers.



WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

Required

 - 5+ years in service design, user experience, product, strategy consulting, change enablement, or a similar client-facing role where you helped people work differently.

 - Demonstrable human-centered design mindset — you start with the work and the humans doing it, not the tool.

 - Hands-on fluency with Claude Cowork, ChatGPT, or similar agentic platforms. You understand prompt design, skill building, and how these tools actually change work. If you've built skills, plugins, or custom workflows, even better.

 - Experience designing and delivering workshops or enablement sessions. You know the difference between a session that felt good and one that changed behavior.

 - Strong written and verbal communication. You can talk to an executive and to an engineer in the same meeting without losing either.

 - Comfort with ambiguity and speed. You ship before it's perfect because you know perfect is just late.

Nice to Have

 - Background in service design, UX research, or human-centered design methodology.

 - Familiarity with Anthropic's ecosystem — Claude Cowork, Claude Code, MCP connectors, skills and plugins.

 - Experience in private equity, financial services, or healthcare. 90% of our training today is at PE-backed companies — domain fluency accelerates trust.

 - Change management or enterprise adoption track record — you know a tool deployment is the beginning, not the end.

 - Experience building or scaling a professional services practice. You've s

source_jobs.title: Private Equity Market Leader
source_jobs.description:
DEPARTMENT Growth

LOCATION Minneapolis, MN / Hybrid

TYPE Full-time

REPORTS TO Chief Growth Officer



Own the PE Market. Build the Vertical. Ship Real AI.

We're building a production-level AI company and we need a Private Equity Market Leader who owns the PE vertical end-to-end. Not someone who manages a territory — someone who builds one. Pipeline, positioning, relationships, revenue. The whole thing.

You'll be Blank Metal's face in private equity. That means knowing how PE firms work, what portfolio companies actually need from AI, and how to get from a first conversation to a signed engagement. You'll close deals, shape how we show up in market, and bring back the buyer intelligence that keeps our go-to-market sharp.



WHAT YOU GET TO DO

Pipeline & Revenue

 - Own $5M+ ARR for the PE vertical — source it, build it, close it

 - Run your own pipeline with support from the partner channel and marketing, but no hand-holding

 - Qualify opportunities fast: assess fit, scope realistically, move or kill

 - Partner with delivery to set clients up for success from day one — no over-promising

Market Positioning & Demand

 - Own how Blank Metal is positioned in PE: what we sell, where we play, what we say no to

 - Understand the PE buyer journey — fund dynamics, portco priorities, how AI budgets get approved

 - Feed market insight back to inform services, pricing, and go-to-market focus

 - Identify where demand is building before it becomes a crowded room

Public Representation & Relationship Building

 - Show up where PE operators, deal teams, and operating partners spend time — events, intros, online presence

 - Build the right relationships in the market to drive Blank Metal’s credibility and deal flow

 - Represent Blank Metal externally in a way that earns credibility with technical and financial buyers alike

 - Serve as the senior point of contact for PE clients throughout the engagement lifecycle

Market Intelligence & Buyer Insights

 - Bring back what you hear: how PE buyers evaluate AI vendors, where objections are real vs. noise

 - Track competitive dynamics in the PE-focused AI services market

 - Deliver buyer intelligence that directly improves our messaging, proposals, and delivery scoping

 - Maintain rigorous pipeline discipline using HubSpot and Notion — you drive outcomes, not log activity

Scope Protection & Escalation

 - Protect deal integrity: set honest expectations and hold the line on scope creep

 - Serve as the escalation point when client relationships need a senior voice

 - Understand what our teams can build and what they can't — and never sell past that line

 - Collaborate with the CGO and delivery leads to ensure what we commit to actually ships



WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

Required

 - 7+ years in enterprise services, business development, or market development — with direct PE market exposure. Working inside a PE firm, portco, or a firm that sold to PE is required to be considered

 - Existing relationships in PE: deal teams, operating partners, C-suite at portfolio companies — not a cold list

 - Track record selling complex professional services or custom build engagements, not SaaS subscriptions

 - Comfortable in technical conversations: you don't need to code, but you need to understand what an AI engineering team actually builds and why it matters to a PE portfolio

 - Entrepreneurial and self-directed — you build the structure, you don't wait for it

 - Strong communicator across financial and technical audiences: you can hold a strategy conversation with a CFO and a scoping conversation with an engineer

 - Data-driven: you track what you own, know what's working, and don't confuse activity with progress

 - Fluent in modern sales tools and whatever you actually use to close deals

Nice to Have

 - Experience working inside or alongside an AI company or technical product environment

 - Familiarity with AI deployment timelines, build vs. buy dynamics, and enterprise AI pro

source_jobs.title: OpenAI Partner Lead
source_jobs.description:
OpenAI Partner Lead

DEPARTMENT Growth

LOCATION Minneapolis, MN or San Francisco, CA / Hybrid

TYPE Full-time

REPORTS TO Chief Growth Officer



Own the OpenAI Partnership. Turn Certifications Into Revenue.

Blank Metal builds and ships production AI. The OpenAI ecosystem is one of our core delivery platforms, and we're hiring an OpenAI Partner Lead to own that relationship like a business — program operations, AE relationships, certifications, co-sell pipeline, and the revenue that comes from doing all of it well.

This isn't a relationship-maintenance role. You'll run Blank Metal's OpenAI partnership with full accountability: compliance, pipeline attribution, FDE coordination, and ensuring every OpenAI-sourced opportunity ends in a shipped, production AI system for the client.

The CGO is currently holding this seat. We're hiring because the OpenAI partnership is large enough to require dedicated ownership.



WHAT YOU GET TO DO

Partner Program Operations & Compliance

 - Own Blank Metal's standing in the OpenAI partner program: certifications, tier requirements, and compliance obligations

 - Manage all reporting cycles and documentation

 - Stay current on OpenAI program changes, new tiers, and co-sell requirements and translate them into action internally

 - Serve as the internal expert on what the OpenAI partnership allows, requires, and offers

Pipeline & Partner-Sourced Revenue

 - Drive significant partner-sourced revenue through the OpenAI channel: build relationships with AEs and make Blank Metal the first call when a client needs an implementation partner

 - Build trackable co-sell motions with OpenAI AEs — structured, attributed, repeatable

 - Qualify OpenAI-referred opportunities fast and move them into the delivery pipeline without friction

 - Own the revenue contribution from the OpenAI channel and report it clearly

Program-Level Partner Relationships

 - Own relationships at multiple levels of OpenAI: AEs, partnership team, solutions architects, and leadership where relevant

 - Ensure Blank Metal has coverage at each level so no single relationship is a single point of failure

 - Represent Blank Metal at OpenAI partner events, summits, and co-marketing opportunities

 - Be the face of Blank Metal inside OpenAI — the team should know who we are and why we're the right partner for their most important enterprise clients

FDE Coordination, Certifications & Solution Design Standards

 - Manage certifications across the Blank Metal team — who's certified, what's lapsing, what's required for deal eligibility

 - Partner with delivery and engineering leadership to staff OpenAI-sourced engagements with certified FDEs, and track certification readiness across the team.

 - Champion OpenAI's recommended architectures and reference patterns inside Blank Metal's engineering standards process, and flag deltas between platform guidance and our delivery patterns.

 - Feed OpenAI product updates — new models, API capabilities, deprecations — back to delivery and sales so we're always current

Partner Adoption Success

 - Track adoption metrics across OpenAI-enabled engagements: which clients are live, which are expanding, which are stalling

 - Work with delivery leads to ensure OpenAI-sourced clients see measurable results — their success is our case study

 - Identify expansion opportunities within existing OpenAI-referred accounts and route them back to the sales motion

 - Document wins and build reusable assets that help OpenAI AEs pitch Blank Metal with confidence



WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

Required

 - 5+ years in a partner, alliance, or channel role at an AI company, system integrator, or technical professional services firm

 - Direct experience working with or inside a hyperscaler, AI platform, or foundation model company partner program

 - Understanding of how enterprise AI partnerships work: co-sell motions, deal registration, tier mechanics, attribution

 - Familiarity with the OpenAI platform, GPT

source_jobs.title: AI Product Leader
source_jobs.description:
DEPARTMENT Product

LOCATION Minneapolis, MN (Hybrid)

TYPE Full time

REPORTS TO Head of Product



THE ROLE

The AI Product Leader sits at the intersection of product delivery, engagement leadership, and applied AI strategy. In practice it looks like: ~40% product delivery, ~40% client leadership, ~20% AI strategy. You will lead 2–3 concurrent engagements and be accountable for shipping, adoption, and measurable impact.



WHAT YOU GET TO DO

1) Product delivery and execution

 - Own backlogs, prioritization, and sprint execution across 2–3 engagements.

 - Run standups, planning, retros, and cross-team coordination with engineering/design/data.

 - Write crisp user stories and acceptance criteria; make trade-offs under real constraints.

 - Ensure production readiness: security, compliance, data governance, deployment, and operational runbooks.

 - Drive post-launch iteration using user feedback, telemetry, and KPI performance.
   
   

2) Client and engagement leadership

 - Lead client working sessions and check-ins; steer decision-making with exec and practitioner stakeholders.

 - Translate ambiguity into clear plans, milestones, and measurable outcomes.

 - Own delivery health: scope, risks, dependencies, and expectation management.

 - Partner with the engagement lead/account team on renewals/expansions and executive readouts (no hard sales quota).
   
   

3) Applied AI strategy (GenAI-heavy)

 - Help clients choose the right approach: workflow redesign, retrieval, tool use, evaluation strategy, rollout.

 - Design evaluation and measurement: rubrics, golden datasets, offline/online testing, and monitoring plans.

 - Make practical architecture-level recommendations with engineers (you don’t have to be the architect).

 - Stay current on model capabilities/limits and translate that into realistic product decisions.
   
   

WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - You have shipped software to production and can point to what you built, what changed, and how it performed.

 - You can run delivery: budget, backlog, sprints, trade-offs, and stakeholder alignment (without hiding behind process).

 - You are fluent in modern GenAI patterns (prompting, RAG basics, evaluation, iteration) and have hands-on experience using tools to build prototypes or workflows.

 - You thrive in ambiguity: starting with incomplete requirements, making decisions with imperfect information, and adapting as you learn.
   
   

What we’ll accept as equivalent

 - Client leadership can be external clients or internal “enterprise clients” where you owned budget, outcomes, timelines, and executive stakeholders.

 - Experience with AI-powered low-code platforms (V0, Bolt, Lovable) for rapid prototyping and stakeholder demos

 - AI experience can be GenAI product work or adjacent data/ML product work plus clear hands-on GenAI experimentation in the last 12 months.
   

Required

 - AI/GenAI Product Manager who has also led enterprise delivery and exec stakeholders.

 - Engagement Manager/Delivery Lead from a SaaS/AI product company who has shipped software and can do product thinking.

 - Senior Product Owner/TPM with strong stakeholder leadership plus clear GenAI build/eval work.
   
   

Nice to have

 - Evaluation harnesses, red-team testing, or model monitoring in production.

 - Regulated environments (healthcare/finance/insurance) and security/compliance constraints.

 - Change management: rollout, enablement, adoption metrics.
   Professional services experience, especially fixed-price or outcome-based delivery.
   

Not a fit if

 - You only want strategy work and don’t want to own shipping, iteration, and operational realities.

 - You prefer low-ambiguity roles with highly mature processes or narrow ownership.

 - You want people management or practice leadership right now.

 - You are remote-only and not able to travel to MSP regularly.



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

Competitive base salary with a revenue-based variable comp structure — when the compa

source_jobs.title: Healthcare Account Leader
source_jobs.description:
DEPARTMENT Growth

LOCATION Minneapolis, MN or San Francisco, CA (Hybrid)

TYPE Full time

REPORTS TO Healthcare Practice Lead



THE ROLE

We're building a production-level AI company, and our healthcare practice is one of the places where the work matters most. We need a Healthcare Account Lead who can sit across the table from a CEO, COO, CIO, or CMO, earn trust through clear thinking, and turn that trust into engagements that compound over time.

In this role you'll own the executive relationships in our healthcare accounts, shape how Blank Metal applies AI to healthcare problems, and carry deals from first conversation through MSA, SOW, and a clean handoff to delivery. You'll work closely with the Head of Healthcare Practice, our engineering and project leads, and our partners at Anthropic, OpenAI and other platforms to ship work that moves the business.

This isn't an account-management role. It's account leadership — strategic, commercial, and hands-on with the AI conversation. Hybrid full-time, based in San Francisco or Minneapolis, reporting to the Head of Healthcare Practice.



WHAT YOU GET TO DO

Executive Relationships

 - Own executive-level client relationships across the C-suite — CEO, COO, CIO, CMO, CFO, and the senior leaders who actually move budget

 - Earn trust through consistent execution and clear thinking, not vendor theater

 - Set the cadence and forum that keep executives close to the work — exec syncs, steering committees, and the right read-outs at the right altitude

 - Use escalation as a tool, not a failure: know when to raise something up the chain, who to bring it to, and how to frame it so the right decision gets made fast

 - Be the single point of accountability across your engagements — the first call when something matters, the steady hand when it gets hard

 - Drive client satisfaction by closing the loop on commitments, surfacing risks early, and protecting the relationship over the transaction

Account Growth & Commercial Ownership

 - Build and execute account growth plans that map directly to the client's clinical, operational, and financial strategy

 - Find the next (few) problems worth solving before the current one wraps

 - Own MSAs and SOWs from first conversation to signature, working tightly with legal, finance, and delivery

 - Drive measurable, attributed account expansion year over year, with disciplined pipeline hygiene in HubSpot and Notion

 - Use AI agents and automations to run accounts — meeting prep, account intelligence, follow-up, expansion signals — and demonstrate the AI-native operating model in how you actually work

Healthcare AI Approach

 - Help define how Blank Metal applies AI across payer, provider, life sciences, and digital health — what we do well, where we win, and what we don't take on

 - Translate healthcare workflows, regulatory realities (HIPAA, HITRUST), and clinical and operational pain into repeatable solution patterns

 - Partner with engineering and solution design to scope the right work for the right outcome — production AI, not pilots that die in slide decks

 - Build the points of view, reference architectures, and case studies that establish our healthcare practice in the market

Sales-to-Delivery Handoff

 - Set engagements up to win on day one: clear scope, clear roles, clear outcomes, realistic timelines

 - Stay engaged through the transition — you don't disappear at signature; you stay close as delivery ramps

 - Partner with delivery leads to keep clients informed, supported, and growing throughout the engagement

 - Bring delivery insights back into pitches, proposals, and roadmap conversations so we sharpen with every project



WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - 7+ years in account leadership, professional services, strategic management consulting, or enterprise sales with senior healthcare clients

 - AI-native operator: you've used AI tools to change how you work and you can show what you built, we will ask you to open your com

source_jobs.title: Account Leader
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

A meaningful part of Blank Metal's growth comes from the clients who already shipped production AI with us and want to do more. The Account Leader owns that opportunity: turning a first engagement into a long-term, expanding partnership.

You'll own a book of existing accounts and grow it. That means staying close to the work our teams ship, spotting where AI can solve the client's next problem, and building the trust that turns one project into the next three. You're the person clients call when something's working and they want more of it, and the person who sees what's possible before they ask.

This is a hands-on role for someone who can sit with an engineer one hour and a CFO the next, and who measures success in production systems and expansion revenue, not meetings booked. You'll build the AI-native account workflows that let a lean team manage deep relationships at scale.



WHAT YOU GET TO DO

Account Growth & Expansion

• Own a portfolio of existing client accounts and a quota for expansion revenue: identify, scope, and close follow-on engagements that deepen each relationship.

• Map every account: know who holds budget, what's running in production, and where the next AI opportunity is before the client raises it.

• Track relationships, expansion pipeline, and account health in HubSpot and Notion, and run a disciplined cadence of check-ins, business reviews, and renewal conversations.

Client Engagement & Storytelling

• Sit close to delivery: understand what each team is building, translate it into outcomes the client's leadership cares about, and turn shipped work into the case for what's next.

• Present Blank Metal's work in a consultative way, through business reviews, proposals, and conversations that connect production results to the next investment.

• Partner with delivery teams to scope follow-on work realistically, and with marketing to turn client wins into stories that drive the next ones.

AI-Native Account Operations

• Conceive and ship AI agents that automate account workflows: expansion-signal detection, account health scoring, renewal tracking, and client communications.

• Own your numbers: expansion revenue, net revenue retention and account health, and build the tooling that makes them visible in real time.

• We're a startup, so you'll build this function, not inherit it. Some weeks that means closing a six-figure expansion; some weeks it means wiring up the automation that tracks it. Both matter.



WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

• 5+ years in account management, expansion sales, or consultative selling, with a track record growing existing accounts in professional services, AI, or technology.

• Proven ability to grow a book of business: you've turned first engagements into multi-year relationships and can point to the expansion revenue to prove it.

• A strong communicator who can navigate a technical conversation with engineers and a strategic one with C-level executives, often in the same day.

• Fluent in BD tools and AI-enabled workflows. Be prepared to show us agents or automations you've built.

• Self-directed and entrepreneurial: you build structure where there isn't any, and you'd rather own a number than manage a process.

• Data-driven: comfortable tracking retention, expansion, and account health, and honest about what the numbers say.



WHY YOU'LL LOVE IT HERE

• Build AI that ships: every project ends in a live deployment, not a slide deck.

• Craft meets velocity: we obsess over quality while delivering at startup speed.

• Outcome-driven model: our work aligns with client success, not billable hours.

• AI-native culture: you'll work with peers who deeply understand modern AI's potential and limits.

• Real collaboration: open conversations, shared ownership, and space to bring your voice.



ABOUT BLANK METAL

Blank Metal is an AI-native engineering company that partners with enterprises to deliver production AI fast. We blend strategic insight with hands-on execu

source_jobs.title: Training Producer
source_jobs.description:
DEPARTMENT Enablement

LOCATION Minneapolis, MN (Hybrid)

TYPE Full time

REPORTS TO Head of Enablement



THE ROLE

When a client signs up for Blank Metal training, the live session is the moment they decide whether we're worth it. A clean run — the Zoom configured right, the chat humming, the breakout rooms landing on time, every trainer knowing exactly what they're on the hook for — is invisible when it works and impossible to hide when it doesn't. Right now that orchestration lives in a few people's heads. We need someone to own it.

As our Training Producer, you are the person who makes each training experience great. You're the single point of contact for everyone involved: Blank Metal trainers, supporting staff, and the client. For everything that isn't the teaching itself. You run the logistics, you run the communications, and you make sure the dozen small things that have to go right actually go right, every time.

This is an early-career role with real ownership from day one. You won't be handed a polished playbook. Instead, you'll help build one. If you like being the calm center of a live event and you take pride in details other people never notice, this is a seat where that instinct matters every single week.


WHAT YOU GET TO DO

Own the run of every training

 - Be the point person for each training — the one Blank Metal and the client both come to for anything logistics-related, before, during, and after the session.

 - Make sure everyone supporting a training knows their role, what's expected of them, and gets their pieces done on time.

 - Run the live session behind the scenes: configure Zoom correctly, work the chat and surface the right questions to the trainers, prep and launch polls, trigger breakout rooms, and quietly round up anyone who gets lost.

 - Open each session with a short, scripted overview of Blank Metal so attendees know who they're learning from.

Own the communication around it

 - Handle the full arc of attendee communication — invitations and Eventbrite setup, expectation-setting before the session, and surveys and follow-ups after.

 - Keep clients clear on what's happening and what we need from them ahead of each training.

 - Set participants up to show up ready, so the live time is spent learning, not troubleshooting setup.

Keep us honest on the details

 - Fulfill partner requirements — for example, providing participant counts to Anthropic for Claude Code trainings.

 - Capture the final participant count and keep the records straight for every session.

 - Be honest-startup scrappy: some of this isn't documented yet, so when you spot a step that lives in someone's head or a process that breaks under pressure, you write it down and fix it instead of working around it.


WHAT WILL HELP YOU SUCCEED

 - Some experience running live or virtual sessions — events, webinars, training, classroom support, AV/production, or anything where the show had to go on while you held it together.

 - You're organized to the point that other people notice. You track the moving pieces and nothing falls through.

 - You stay calm when something breaks live, and you fix it without the audience ever knowing.

 - You write clearly and warmly — the kind of attendee email people actually read and a client note that reassures.

 - You're comfortable owning a checklist that doesn't fully exist yet, and you'd rather build the missing piece than complain about it.

 - Bonus: you've used Zoom (breakouts, polls), Eventbrite, or survey tools, and you're genuinely curious about AI — you'll be in the room for every Claude training we run.


WHY YOU'LL LOVE IT HERE

 - Build AI that ships: every project ends in a live deployment, not a slide deck

 - Craft meets velocity: we obsess over quality while delivering at startup speed

 - Outcome-driven model: our work aligns with client success, not billable hours

 - AI-native culture: you'll work with peers who deeply understand modern AI's potential and limits
